markdown.markdown:
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 8
%%| fig-height: 2.5
flowchart LR
    A((Objects)) --> B((Functions)) --> C((Operators))
    linkStyle default stroke:#adb5bd,stroke-width:2px
```


The core of R is creating objects using functions and operators. Before
anything more advanced it is important to have a good understanding of
objects, functions, and operators. You will get plenty of practice using
these concepts in the other tutorials and in class.

## Knowledge Check

The knowledge checks are required for each class. Wrong answers are not
counted against you. Complete all questions below, then submit your
results to your instructor.

::: {.quiz data-id="kc1" data-type="single"}
**What does the assignment operator `<-` do?**

- [ ] Compares two values
- [ ] Deletes an object
- [x] Creates an object and assigns it a value
- [ ] Prints output to the console
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc2" data-type="single"}
**What is the output of the following code?** `x <- c(10, 20, 30)` then `class(x)`

- [ ] "data.frame"
- [x] "numeric"
- [ ] "character"
- [ ] "vector"
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc3" data-type="single"}
**Which of the following is a valid object name in R?**

- [ ] `2nd_attempt`
- [ ] `my variable`
- [x] `budget_total`
- [ ] `data!set`
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc4" data-type="single"}
**What does `str(my_data)` tell you?**

- [ ] The sum of all values
- [ ] Only the class of the object
- [ ] The first 6 rows
- [x] The structure: class, dimensions, variable names, and classes
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc5" data-type="single"}
**What is the result of `10 != 10` in R?**

- [x] FALSE
- [ ] TRUE
- [ ] An error
- [ ] 0
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc6" data-type="single"}
**You run `mean(my_df$revenue)` and store the result with `avg_rev <- mean(my_df$revenue)`. What is `avg_rev`?**

- [ ] A function
- [ ] A dataframe
- [x] A single numeric value (an object)
- [ ] A vector
:::

::: {.quiz data-id="kc7" data-type="single"}
**Which operator would you use to check if city population is greater than or equal to 50,000?**

- [ ] `>`
- [ ] `==`
- [x] `>=`
- [ ] `!=`
:::

## Conclusion

The core of R is creating objects using functions and operators. Before
anything more advanced it is important to have a good understanding of
objects, functions, and operators. You will get plenty of practice using
these concepts in the other tutorials and in class.

## Knowledge Check
